key_insight: A high-selectivity, non-metallic deep ICP-RIE process can fully etch 16 μm single-crystal diamond membranes and then enable room-temperature bonding onto silica for diamond-on-silica heterostructures.
engineering_relevance: This offers a scalable, contamination-free fabrication route for integrated diamond photonics and quantum sensing devices using commercially accessible substrates and low-temperature bonding.
method: Single-layer SiO2 hard mask with multi-step oxygen ICP-RIE
Diamond-to-SiO2 etch selectivity of 15:1 for deep, well-defined features
O2 plasma activation plus sodium silicate interlayer for room-temperature bonding
evidence_signal: The abstract reports complete through-etching of a 16 μm NV-doped diamond membrane, preserved surface roughness, negligible micromasking, and optically transparent bonded films with no detectable parasitic photoluminescence.
watch_item: The process depends on precise plasma etch control and reliable oxide/silicate surface preparation, which may affect yield and reproducibility at larger scales.
paper_title: Scalable Fabrication of Diamond-on-Silica Heterostructures via High-Selectivity Deep ICP-RIE and Room-Temperature Bonding
